chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No. D- 2183 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For order on MA No 8153/2017 2. For orders on office objections 3. For order on MA No 8154/2017 4. For katcha Peshi 01.02.2018. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto Asstt:A.G a/w Inspector Manzoor Ali on behalf of DIG Hyderabad & PI S.Manzoor Ali Shah on behalf of SSP Hyderabad -=-=- This is second call. Neither petitioner nor his counsel is present. No intimation is received. It is now 12.10 pm. Learned Asstt:A.G files comments of respondents No.2,3,4,5 & 7 which are taken on record. Since none is present on behalf of the petitioner, therefore, we ourselves have gone through the memo of petition as well as prayer clause of the petition. It appears from the record that petitioner has sought the following prayers:- a) That this Honourable court may be pleased to direct the respondents NO.4,5 & 6 to arrest the absconding accused and take legal action against them in accordance with law. b) To direct the respondent No.4 to provide legal protection to the petitioner and his family members form the hands of absconding accused persons. c) Take lawful action against the official respondents No.6, 7 & 8 and departmental inquiry may be conducted against them for their intentional negligence in the light of order of learned civil Judge and Judicial Magistrate-III Hyderabad dated 20.1.2017 d) That, this Honourable court may also be pleased to impose a penalty of Rs. One Million upon the official respondent’s No.6,7 & 8 for their intentional and wilful negligence and same may be paid to the petitioner as compensation. e) Any other relief which this Honourable court deems fit and proper Learned Asstt: A.G present and submits that official respondents would act strictly in accordance with law.

It appears from the record that petitioner has sought the following prayers:- a) That this Honourable court may be pleased to direct the respondents NO.4,5 & 6 to arrest the absconding accused and take legal action against them in accordance with law. b) To direct the respondent No.4 to provide legal protection to the petitioner and his family members form the hands of absconding accused persons. c) Take lawful action against the official respondents No.6, 7 & 8 and departmental inquiry may be conducted against them for their intentional negligence in the light of order of learned civil Judge and Judicial Magistrate-III Hyderabad dated 20.1.2017 d) That, this Honourable court may also be pleased to impose a penalty of Rs. One Million upon the official respondent’s No.6,7 & 8 for their intentional and wilful negligence and same may be paid to the petitioner as compensation. e) Any other relief which this Honourable court deems fit and proper Learned Asstt: A.G present and submits that official respondents would act strictly in accordance with law. In view of the undertaking of Learned Asstt: A.G this petition is disposed of alongwith listed applications with direction to the respondents that they would act strictly in accordance with law. So for as the prayer clauses (a), (c) & (d) are concerned, same do not come within the ambit of this court as such same are not entertained. JUDGE JUDGE A.Rasheed
